statement.markdown: In this task, you will be given a Docker container configuration as input. Your task is to fix the port mapping configuration to ensure that the container exposes the correct ports to the host system. The input will be a string representing the Docker container configuration. The output should be the corrected port mapping configuration as a string.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a string representing the Docker container configuration. The string will be in the format: `docker run -p <host_port>:<container_port> <image_name>`
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the corrected port mapping configuration as a string.
statement.notes: You should ensure that the container exposes the correct ports to the host system.

constraints.description: The number of ports exposed by the container

examples.explanation: The container exposes ports 80 and 81. However, the port mapping configuration is incorrect. The corrected configuration ensures that the container exposes the correct ports to the host system.
The container exposes port 8080. However, the port mapping configuration is incorrect. The corrected configuration ensures that the container exposes port 80 to the host system.

hints.text: Check the port mapping configuration for correctness.

editorial.title: Docker Port Mapping Fixer
editorial.intuition: The container exposes ports to the host system using the -p flag. The format of the port mapping configuration is <host_port>:<container_port>.
editorial.bruteForce: You can manually check each port mapping configuration to ensure that it is correct.
editorial.betterSolution: You can write a program to parse the input and correct the port mapping configuration.
editorial.optimalSolution: You can use a regular expression to parse the input and correct the port mapping configuration.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The corrected port mapping configuration ensures that the container exposes the correct ports to the host system.
editorial.dryRun: You can test the corrected port mapping configuration by running the container and checking the exposed ports.
